data.title: Our puzzle
data.solution:
import re

sentence = input()
match = re.search(r"(?:^|[^[A-Za-z])[Mm][Yy]\s+([A-Za-z]+)", sentence)
assert match is not None

print(f"Our {match[1]}.")
data.statement:
« If you have an original idea, if it's good, it won't be yours for long. » -- hbomberguy, Plagiarism and You(Tube)

You are provided with a sentence from someone declaring a [[thing]] as theirs, by saying {{my}} (case-insensitive), followed by spaces, then a single-word [[thing]].

You must extract the [[thing]], then declare shared ownership by presenting it with the following format:

{{Our }}[[thing]]{{.}}

You must keep the original casing of the [[thing]].

In this context, words are sets of letters from A to Z (case-insensitive). There will never be a case where {{my}} is followed by something else than a space then a [[thing]].

testCases.title: Lowercase adjective + thing, no punctuation
testCases.testIn: You will find my fridge next to the oven.

testCases.testIn: It is my pleasure to welcome you to this community.

testCases.title: Lowercase adjective + thing, followed by punctuation
testCases.testIn: I think you will find that this was my invention.

testCases.title: Mynd games
testCases.testIn: Mysterious how my mind is able to define myself!

testCases.title: Mynd games
testCases.testIn: It's either mythology or my life, following the myopathy diagnosis that was given to me.

testCases.title: In the myddle
testCases.testIn: The emyd ended up eating my salad... or is it emyde?

testCases.title: In the myddle
testCases.testIn: The amygdala is likely responsible for my sadness, or is it not knowing how to write geomytri.

testCases.title: Or an enemy
testCases.testIn: Tommy's tummy is filled with my candy, he might end up chummy or worse, happy!

testCases.title: Or an enemy
testCases.testIn: We aim for autonomy, my hope is that we will gain full control of our economy in a year!

lastVersion.statementHTML:
Goal

« If you have an original idea, if it's good, it won't be yours for long. » -- hbomberguy, Plagiarism and You(Tube)

You are provided with a sentence from someone declaring a thing as theirs, by saying my (case-insensitive), followed by spaces, then a single-word thing.

You must extract the thing, then declare shared ownership by presenting it with the following format:

Our thing.

You must keep the original casing of the thing.

In this context, words are sets of letters from A to Z (case-insensitive). There will never be a case where my is followed by something else than a space then a thing.
Input
First line: sentence
Output
First line: Our thing.
Constraints
4 ≤ length(sentence) < 99
sentence is ASCII-encoded.
Example
Input
You will find my fridge next to the oven.
Output
Our fridge.
